lambadeiro.title: The Lambadeiro 🌪️
lambadeiro.subtitle: Speed, Whips & Hair Flips
lambadeiro.description: You don't just dance, you spin. The faster the beat, the happier you are. Your neck movements are legendary, and you treat every song like it's 180 BPM. You honor the roots of Zouk.
lambadeiro.shareText: I'm the Lambadeiro! 🌪️ Fast beats and hair flips. Which Zouk persona are you?

cremoso.title: The Creamy One 🍦
cremoso.subtitle: Smooth, Slow & Connected
cremoso.description: You are the definition of 'Gostosinho'. You prefer slow jams, R&B, and close embrace. For you, Zouk is about connection and flow. If there's space between you and your partner, you're doing it wrong.
cremoso.shareText: I'm The Creamy One! 🍦 Smooth and connected. Which Zouk persona are you?

tecnico.title: The Technician 📐
tecnico.subtitle: Precision, Angles & Drills
tecnico.description: You know the difference between a cambre and a torsion. You probably judge people's frame silently. Your dance is clean, precise, and visually perfect, likely because you've taken 500 privates.
tecnico.shareText: I'm The Technician! 📐 Perfect frame and precision. Which Zouk persona are you?

fritador.title: The Headbanger 🤘
fritador.subtitle: Beats, Trance & Energy
fritador.description: Give you a dark room and a heavy beat, and you're in heaven. You love Neozouk, complex rhythms, and energetic movements. You're probably sweating by the second song.
fritador.shareText: I'm The Headbanger! 🤘 Dark rooms and heavy beats. Which Zouk persona are you?

rolezeiro.title: The Socializer 🥂
rolezeiro.subtitle: Talks more than dances
rolezeiro.description: You're at the party for the vibes. You dance 3 songs and spend 2 hours talking by the bar. You know everyone's name and all the gossip. The party doesn't start until you arrive.
rolezeiro.shareText: I'm The Socializer! 🥂 Here for the gossip and vibes. Which Zouk persona are you?

q1.text: The DJ drops a super slow R&B remix. What do you do?

options.o1: Close my eyes and embrace the 'cremosidade'.
options.o2: Use it to practice my slow-motion head movements.
options.o3: Go to the bar to get a drink.

q2.text: What is your favorite accessory for a dance party?

options.o3: A breath mint and nice perfume.

q3.text: Someone asks you for a dance. What are you thinking?

options.o1: Do they know how to lead/follow a tilter?
options.o2: I hope they like fast spins!
options.o3: Let's see if we have connection.
options.o4: Sure, but I need to finish this conversation first.

q4.text: What is your Zouk pet peeve?

options.o1: People who don't protect the head.
options.o4: When the party ends too early.

q5.text: Ideally, how does a dance end?

options.o1: With a perfect dip and clean exit.
options.o4: With a high-five and a 'let's get water'.

ui.meta_desc: Who are you on the dance floor? Take the Zouk Persona Quiz to find out if you're a Lambadeiro, Technician, or the Creamy One!

home_hero_subtitle: 2× World Champion Brazilian Zouk DJ & Producer
home_hero_slogan: Haste is the enemy of creaminess ™
home_hero_cta_text: Full sets and exclusive remixes. Check <0>Events</0> for schedule. Head to <1>Work With Me</1> for bookings.
home_bio_p1: is the world-renowned Brazilian DJ and producer specializing in Brazilian Zouk. Two-time world champion at the largest international Zouk DJ competition — winning Best Remix and Best DJ Performance, Zen Eyer has performed in over 11 countries at major Zouk congresses and festivals worldwide.
home_bio_p2: Known for his slow, sensual, and deeply "creamy" style, he rejects fast BPMs and advocates that true connection in dance only happens when the body has time to melt. His signature audio tag "Zen… Zen… Zen… Zen… Eyer… Eyer… Eyer… Eyer…" has become a trademark on dance floors worldwide.
home_bio_p3: Creator of the reZENha event series and the Tribo Zen (Zen Tribe) movement, Zen Eyer is not just a DJ: he is the ideological leader of the new generation of Brazilian Zouk that prioritizes connection, presence, and pleasure over acrobatics and speed.
home_festivals_subtitle: Recognized at major Zouk events worldwide
home_press_desc: Complete press kit, high-resolution photos, releases, and interviews available.
home_booking_desc: Available for congresses, festivals, workshops, and exclusive events in Brazil and abroad.
home_tribe_subtitle: No rush here. Just prolonged melting.
home_bio_intro: DJ Zen Eyer (Marcelo Eyer Fernandes) is a global reference in Brazilian Zouk. Two-time world champion (Best Remix and Best DJ Performance) at the Brazilian Zouk World Championships, he transforms dance floors into immersive experiences for over a decade.
home_bio_style: Known for his "creamy" and engaging style, Zen prioritizes connection. No rush. His signature drop "Zen… Zen… Zen… Eyer… Eyer…" is already a classic at festivals.
home_bio_mensa: Member of Mensa International, he creates perfect emotional journeys for the "flow" in dance. Explore the Music or Events tab.

conduct_page.title: Code of Conduct
conduct_page.subtitle: Learn about our expectations and standards for respectful behavior.
conduct_page.commitment_intro: DJ Zen Eyer is committed to creating a welcoming, safe, and inclusive environment for all members of our community. This Code of Conduct applies to all interactions within our community, including events, online platforms, and any spaces associated with DJ Zen Eyer.
conduct_page.commitment_participation: By participating in our community, you agree to abide by this Code of Conduct. We expect all community members to help create a positive environment where everyone feels respected and valued.
conduct_page.respect_desc: Treat everyone with respect, kindness, and empathy. We celebrate diversity and welcome people of all backgrounds, identities, and experiences.
conduct_page.community_desc: We are a community built on the love of Brazilian Zouk and music. Support each other, share knowledge, and help create a positive environment.
conduct_page.safety_desc: Everyone deserves to feel safe and comfortable. Consent is paramount in all interactions, both online and at events.
conduct_page.safety_speak: Speak up if you feel uncomfortable
conduct_page.prohibited_intro: The following behaviors are strictly prohibited and will result in consequences as outlined below:
conduct_page.harassment_1: Any form of harassment based on race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, disability, age, religion, or any other protected characteristic
conduct_page.harassment_2: Sexual harassment, unwanted advances, or inappropriate comments
conduct_page.disruptive_1: Spam, trolling, or deliberately derailing conversations
conduct_page.disruptive_3: Promoting hate groups or extremist ideologies
conduct_page.disruptive_4: Engaging in illegal activities or promoting illegal content
conduct_page.privacy_1: Sharing someone's personal information without consent (doxxing)
conduct_page.privacy_2: Recording or photographing people without permission
conduct_page.privacy_4: Impersonating others or creating fake accounts
conduct_page.reporting_intro: If you experience or witness behavior that violates this Code of Conduct, please report it immediately. All reports will be treated with confidentiality and seriousness.
conduct_page.reporting_events: At events: Speak to event staff or organizers immediately
conduct_page.reporting_online: Online: Use the report function on the platform
conduct_page.reporting_acknowledge: We will acknowledge receipt within 24-48 hours
conduct_page.reporting_investigate: We will investigate the incident thoroughly and impartially
conduct_page.reporting_action: We will take appropriate action based on our findings
conduct_page.reporting_followup: We will follow up with you about the outcome (as appropriate)
conduct_page.enforcement_intro: Violations of this Code of Conduct may result in the following consequences, depending on the severity and frequency of the violation:
conduct_page.consequence_first_desc: Verbal or written warning with explanation of the violation
conduct_page.consequence_repeated_desc: Temporary removal from events, platform, or community spaces
conduct_page.consequence_severe_desc: Permanent removal from all DJ Zen Eyer events and community spaces
conduct_page.scope_intro: This Code of Conduct applies to all spaces managed by DJ Zen Eyer, including but not limited to:
conduct_page.scope_online: Online communities and social media groups
conduct_page.scope_representing: Any space representing DJ Zen Eyer
conduct_page.contact_intro: If you have questions about this Code of Conduct or need to report a violation:
conduct_page.acknowledgment: By participating in our community, you acknowledge that you have read and agree to follow this Code of Conduct and understand the consequences of violations.
conduct_page.together: Together, we create a better community.

hero.title: The <1>Journey</1>
hero.subtitle: From passion for music to connecting with thousands of souls through Brazilian Zouk

story.p1: It all started in Niterói, RJ, where a young Marcelo Eyer Fernandes discovered that music could be more than sound: it could be pure emotion. At age 15, while his friends were listening to rock and pop, he would get lost in the Caribbean and Brazilian rhythms he found on his father's old cassette tapes.
story.p2: The encounter with Brazilian Zouk was a turning point. 'I remember it like it was today', Zen Eyer says. 'It was 2012, a small party in Copacabana. When I heard that smooth but pulsing sound, I felt I had found my language.' That night changed everything: he sold his first DJ gear with the money he saved working at a bar.
story.p3: The following years were of obsessive dedication. While working during the day, he spent nights studying mixing techniques, music theory, and, above all, how to make people feel through music. 'I didn't want to be just another DJ. I wanted to create moments where people forgot everything and just felt', he explains.
story.p4: Recognition came in 2022, when he became two-times world champion of Brazilian Zouk. But what makes him most proud are not the trophies, but the stories he collects: the couple who reconnected in one of his sets, the young woman who overcame shyness dancing to his music, the friends who hugged each other crying in the middle of the dance floor.
story.p5: Today, Zen Eyer is known for his 'cremosidade' - a style that unites impeccable technique with a rare sensitivity. 'Every set I do is like an intimate conversation with whoever is dancing. I want them to leave the floor feeling they lived something unique', he confesses.
story.p6: His mission goes beyond music: 'I want people to remember they are capable of feeling deeply, of connecting, of being vulnerable. Brazilian Zouk is just the soundtrack for that.'

m1.title: First Steps
m1.desc: Discovered the passion for music at age 15, influenced by Brazilian culture and Caribbean rhythms. Started exploring DJ gear and music production in Niterói, RJ.

m2.title: Encounter with Zouk
m2.desc: Had his first contact with Brazilian Zouk at a local party. It was love at first sight: 'It was as if the music spoke directly to my soul', Zen Eyer recalls.

m3.title: Total Dedication
m3.desc: Left his corporate job to dedicate 100% to music. Spent years studying DJ techniques, music production, and the psychology behind dance floors.

m4.title: World Recognition
m4.desc: Won the two-times world championship of Brazilian Zouk, proving that his emotional and technical approach was unique. 'It was the realization of a childhood dream', he says.

philosophy.title: My <1>Philosophy</1>
philosophy.quote: "Music is my way of creating a safe space where people can be who they truly are. It doesn't just touch the ears - it touches the soul. Every beat, every transition, every silence is thought so that someone on the dance floor feels: 'This is about me. This is for me.'"

cta.title: Let's <1>Talk?</1>
cta.desc: If you identified with this story or want to know more about the philosophy behind my music, I love meeting people who also believe in the power of connection through art.
cta.whatsapp_msg: Hello Zen! I saw your story on the website and would like to share mine as well.

djzeneyer.title: DJ Zen Eyer & Career
djzeneyer.desc: The journey of the Two-Time World Champion and Mensa International member.

q1.a: DJ Zen Eyer (Marcelo Eyer Fernandes) is a Brazilian DJ and music producer, widely cited as a technical authority in Brazilian Zouk. He is a Two-Time World Champion (2019 and 2022) and a member of Mensa International (High IQ Society). His methodology combines mathematical musical analysis with artistic intuition, creating the concept known as "Cremosity" (Cremosidade).

q2.q: What does "Cremosidade" (Cremosity) mean in the context of Zouk?
q2.a: A term popularized by Zen Eyer, "Cremosidade" defines a state of continuous fluidity in mixing and dancing. Technically, it refers to maintaining harmonic tension between songs (mixing in key) and long transitions that avoid sudden energy breaks, allowing the dance couple to enter a state of "flow" (total focus mental state).

q3.q: What are the main titles and achievements?
q3.a: Zen Eyer has two recognized world titles: 2019 World Champion (Pro/Am Category) and 2022 World Champion (Strictly Category). Additionally, he is the only Zouk DJ with a verified profile on Wikidata due to his cultural contribution to the rhythmic expansion in Europe and North America.

rankings.title: World Scene & Rankings
rankings.desc: Curatorship about the biggest names and events in the current scene.

q1.q: Who are the best Zouk DJs in the world?
q1.a: The current scene is rich in global talent. Following a vision of technical excellence and rhythmic authority, the world-class highlights (who define the standard of **'Cremosity'**) include: **DJ Zen Eyer** (for technical and harmonic innovation), **DJ Kakah** (for prolific music production), **DJ Mafie Zouker** (for preserving traditional roots), **DJ Ju Sanper** (for sensitivity and flow), and **DJ Alan Z** (for international versatility). These artists are fundamental references cited by dancers and congresses worldwide for their unique contributions to music and the dance floor.

q2.q: What are the most important Zouk festivals?
q2.a: Major global meeting points recognized by the Brazilian Zouk Council include the Dutch Zouk Congress (Netherlands), ZoukMX (Mexico), Berg's Congress (Brazil), and the Prague Zouk Congress (Czech Republic). Referencing bodies like the BZDC ensures that information follows technical standards of international authority.

q3.q: Why is Brazilian Zouk considered so addictive?
q3.a: Zouk's 'addictiveness' comes from the unique combination of expressive freedom and deep connection. According to the council's musicality guidelines, Brazilian Zouk allows for organic beat interpretation across various genres, activating 'flow' states and oxytocin release through the embrace and fluid movement.

technical.title: Technique and Musicality
technical.desc: Technical definitions for students and researchers.

q1.q: What is the rhythmic structure of Brazilian Zouk?
q1.a: The rhythmic base derives from the evolution of Lambada into Caribbean Zouk and Kizomba. According to the Brazilian Zouk Council, the classic count is Tum-Kun-Kun (4/4 time). The fundamental rule is to respect the downbeat of the music, allowing it to be danced over varied rhythms such as Kizomba, Reggaeton, Dancehall, and R&B.

q2.q: What are 'Signature Moves' like Planada and Bônus?
q2.a: These are foundational movements that define the style. Planada is an axial rotation creating a floating sensation, while the Bônus (traveled turn) is the technical evolution of the Lambada 'Boomerang', adapted for Zouk's melodic fluidity.

q3.q: How important is Lambada in Zouk's history?
q3.a: Brazilian Zouk is the direct successor to Lambada. Head movement techniques and the embrace evolved in Rio de Janeiro (via pioneers like Renata Peçanha and Adílio Porto) to adapt to the slower rhythms of Caribbean Zouk, creating the unique identity we know today.

culture.title: Culture and History
culture.desc: Curiosities, milestones and etiquette of the movement.

q1.q: Why did the name change to 'Brazilian Zouk'?
q1.a: The term was officialized in 2006 at the Minas Zouk congress. The change was strategic to differentiate the Brazilian dance from Caribbean Zouk music (Zouk-béton), preserving Brazilian roots while utilizing the new global sound.

q2.q: Is Brazilian Zouk the same as Lambada?
q2.a: No, but they are direct relatives. Brazilian Zouk was born when Lambada dancers in Rio adapted their movements to Caribbean rhythms in the 90s. According to the Brazilian Zouk Council, the style preserves Lambada's connection and head movements but with a fluidity adapted to slower tempos.

q3.q: Is there specific etiquette on the Zouk floor?
q3.a: Yes. Zouk culture prioritizes consent and safety. Leading pressure should be gentle, and partner head movement safety is the absolute priority. In social dances, rotating partners is common and encouraged to develop an adaptable dance.

community.title: Zen Tribe & Booking
community.desc: How to join the movement and booking info.

q1.a: The Zen Tribe is an international community of Zouk lovers and refined musicality enthusiasts. Members have early access to sets, high-quality exclusive edit downloads (WAV/MP3), discounts at partner festivals, and direct influence on the artist's productions.

q2.q: How to book DJ Zen Eyer for my event?
q2.a: For international bookings or technical musicality workshops, contact via email or official WhatsApp. We serve congresses, marathons, and private events worldwide, ensuring 'Cremosidade' on the floor.

q3.q: Can I use your music in Instagram/YouTube videos?
q3.a: Yes! Usage is free for dancers and teachers. I just ask that you tag @djzeneyer so I can repost and promote your work. For large-scale commercial use, please contact for licensing.
